title: GDP per capita
subtitle: This data is expressed in US dollars. It is adjusted for inflation but does not account for differences in living costs between countries.
note: This data is expressed in constant 2015 US$.
caption:
GDP per capita

This data is expressed in US dollars. It is adjusted for inflation but does not account for differences in living costs between countries.

This choropleth world map displays GDP per capita for the year 2024, expressed in constant 2015 US dollars. The legend ranges from $0 to $50,000, with color bands marking thresholds at $1,000, $2,000, $5,000, $10,000, and $20,000. The darkest blue shades, representing values above $50,000, are visible in the United States, Canada, Australia, Norway, and Switzerland. Western European nations, including the United Kingdom, France, and Germany, appear in dark blue bands that sit between the $20,000 and $50,000 thresholds. Russia and China are depicted in mid-range blue shades, falling within the $10,000 to $20,000 band. In South America, Brazil and Argentina sit in lighter blue-green shades corresponding to the $5,000 to $10,000 range, while surrounding countries appear in lighter bands. Across Africa, the majority of countries are colored in the lightest shades of green, indicating values below $5,000, with some nations appearing near the $1,000 or $2,000 marks. India and parts of Southeast Asia also display these lighter green shades. Greenland and a few small island territories show a hatched pattern indicating no data. The visual distribution places the highest values in North America, Western Europe, and Oceania, with progressively lighter shades appearing across Asia, South America, and Africa.
